title: 5 Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP)
body_text:
5	Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP)
5.1	Overview of Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP)
Central 1 does not provide support for scripts or the FTP software used to connect to the File and Report Exchange, but it does support the connection. Contact the FTP software manufacturer for FTP software related questions.
Note:	Business member users who require access to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) must use the procedures in Accessing the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP) â Business Member Procedures, Form 3431.
5.2	Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP)
The procedures in this section are generic due to variables in the FTP software used to connect to the File and Report Exchange.
To exchange files on the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP):
Start the FTP Client application.
Select
SFTP Connectivity
as the server type.
Enter
âftpx.central1.com
â as the Host.
Enter â
22
â as the Port.
Enter your user ID and password. Access to the File and Report Exchange is provided once the credentials are authenticated. Users only have access to the folders to which they are assigned. See Chapter 7, File and Report Exchange Folder Structure, for information on the file structure.
Download or upload files as required. Specific upload and download commands are defined by the FTP Client application.
Important
:	Ensure that the file is uploaded to the correct folder. For example, AFT files must be uploaded to the AFT folder. If the file isnât uploaded to the correct folder, Central 1 will not process the file. Information on uploading specific files, including the folder name, is provided in the procedures on the relevant product or service. See the appropriate Part in the Payment Solutions Manual.
Note:	Files are available on the File and Report Exchange for 30 calendar days. The reports from the CRF* and ICR1 files will be purged after seven calendar days since these are advance versions of existing Canadian AFT clearing reports. It is a good practice to download and save files daily.
END
